stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 20 patients. The sample mean is 352.1 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 96.9 mmHg. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 352.1 mmHg. 95% CI = [306.7, 397.5]. Point estimate: 352.1 mmHg.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
